reading.title: Reading Comprehension Questions
reading.text: Sección extraída correctamente. (Revisa el PDF si la pregunta hace referencia a un pasaje largo).

opciones: (A) Why young women chose to move from farms to cities
(B) The number and types of manufacturing jobs created in cities
(C) Changes in settlement patterns between farms and cities
(D) The effects of mechanization on western migration
questions.pregunta: 1. What aspect of the United States in the late nineteenth and early twentieth centuries does the passage mainly discuss?

opciones: (A) change
(B) growth
(C) population
(D) labor
questions.pregunta: 2. The word “expansion” in line 1 is closest in meaning to

opciones: (A) left behind
(B) sold
(C) gave away
(D) gradually reduced the size of
questions.pregunta: 3. The word “abandoned” in line 3 is closest in meaning to

opciones: (A) To indicate that more than one factor contributed to the movement from rural to urban settlement
(B) To support the idea that many families were able to stop farming and migrate to the cities
(C) To suggest that the migration would have happened despite the economic situation
(D) To argue against economics as the primary factor in migration
questions.pregunta: 4. Why does the author discuss “prosperity” in line 4 ?

opciones: (A) city businessmen seeking new investments
(B) successful landowners who had mechanized farms
(C) young farm men starting to work
(D) people who had few opportunities in cities
questions.pregunta: 5. According to the first paragraph, in the late 1800s, farmland was most likely to be purchased by

opciones: (A) unexpectedly
(B) usually
(C) unavoidably
(D) possibly
questions.pregunta: 6. The word “inevitably” in line 12 is closest in meaning to

opciones: (A) industrial workers becoming farmers
(B) the economic development of the countryside
(C) the loss of rural population
(D) innovations in manufacturing
questions.pregunta: 7. The phrase “this trend” in line 14 refers to

opciones: (A) Fewer people lived on farms.
(B) The central part of the United States lost much of its population to rural areas in the West.
(C) Rural areas of the West became more populated than those in the central part of the country.
(D) Work opportunities in urban areas began to decline. 42
questions.pregunta: 8. According to the first paragraph, which of the following is true about the population of the United States after the 1880s?

opciones: (A) Young women were more likely to leave rural areas to go to urban areas.
(B) Young women were more likely to take jobs on farms.
(C) Young women who migrated generally had more education.
(D) Young women generally moved more frequently from one rural area to another.
questions.pregunta: 9. According to the second paragraph, how did migration among young women in the rural West differ from that of young men?

opciones: (A) unappealing
(B) reduced
(C) low-paying
(D) disappearing
questions.pregunta: 10. The word “constricted” in line 24 is closest in meaning to

opciones: (A) The similarities between Earth’s inner core and outer core
(B) The structure and temperature of Earth’s interior
(C) When seismic waves were first used to study Earth’s interior
(D) Why Earth’s solid inner core is surrounded by a molten outer core
questions.pregunta: 11. What does the passage mainly discuss?

opciones: (A) approximate
(B) insignificant
(C) measured
(D) lengthy
questions.pregunta: 12. The word “mere” in line 2 is closest in meaning to

opciones: (A) Earth’s gravitational field
(B) Earth’s magnetic field
(C) vibrations caused by earthquakes
(D) material taken from holes drilled into Earth’s crust
questions.pregunta: 13. According to the first paragraph, most knowledge of Earth’s interior has been gained by studying

opciones: (A) Neither the inner core nor the outer core can be studied using seismic waves.
(B) The outer core is more solid than the inner core.
(C) The inner core and the outer core have greatly different temperatures.
(D) Both the inner core and the outer core probably contain iron.
questions.pregunta: 14. According to the second paragraph, which of the following statements regarding Earth’s inner core and outer core is true?

opciones: (A) basically
(B) similarly
(C) potentially
(D) completely
questions.pregunta: 15. The word “thoroughly” in line 15 is closest in meaning to

opciones: (A) Earth
(B) a cool crust
(C) the molten ball of rock
(D) the heat
questions.pregunta: 16. The word “it” in line 18 refers to

opciones: (A) acted
(B) moved
(C) appeared
(D) grew
questions.pregunta: 17. The word “functioned” in line 18 is closest in meaning to

opciones: (A) changed
(B) combined
(C) utilized
(D) made
questions.pregunta: 18. The word “manufactured” in line 20 is closest in meaning to

opciones: (A) The decay of radioactive isotopes
(B) The movement of elements from Earth’s crust to its interior
(C) The vibrations that result from movement of Earth’s crust
(D) The injection of certain organic elements into Earth’s inner core
questions.pregunta: 19. The third paragraph mentions which of the following as one cause of the current high temperature of Earth’s interior?

opciones: (A) seismic waves
(B) earthquakes
(C) magnetic field
(D) stable elements
questions.pregunta: 20. Look at the terms “seismic waves” (line 6), “earthquakes” (line 6), “magnetic field” (line 14), and “stable elements” (line 21). Which of these terms is defined in the passage?

opciones: (A) the crust affects Earth’s internal temperature
(B) radioactive isotopes cause heat
(C) Earth’s magnetic field is created
(D) seismic waves originate
questions.pregunta: 21. According to the passage, scientists do not understand in detail how

opciones: (A) difference between amber and mineral fossils
(B) places where amber can be found
(C) various characteristics of amber
(D) types of trees that produced amber
questions.pregunta: 22. The passage mainly discusses the

opciones: (A) To emphasize the age of fossils
(B) To explain why there are more mineral replacement fossils than organic fossils
(C) To compare them with amber as a different type of fossil
(D) To give an example of an object in which amber is commonly found
questions.pregunta: 23. Why does the author mention dinosaur bones in the first paragraph?

opciones: (A) beautiful
(B) unusual
(C) obvious
(D) unchanged

opciones: (A) unknown
(B) decreasing
(C) very small
(D) somewhat similar
questions.pregunta: 25. The word “trace” in line 11 is closest in meaning to

opciones: (A) There is a limited number of large deposits of amber in the world.
(B) Most deposits contain large quantities of amber specimens.
(C) The major amber deposits were formed at approximately the same time.
(D) The deposits were formed primarily by tropical broad-leaved trees.
questions.pregunta: 26. According to the second paragraph, which of the following is true about deposits of amber?

opciones: (A) false belief
(B) known fact
(C) scientific theory
(D) subject of debate
questions.pregunta: 27. The word “misconception” in line 16 is closest in meaning to

opciones: (A) deposits
(B) pine trees
(C) conifer trees
(D) broad-leaved trees
questions.pregunta: 28. The word “them” in line 18 refers to

opciones: (A) consequently
(B) instead
(C) moreover
(D) initially
questions.pregunta: 29. The word “Thus” in line 23 is closest in meaning to

opciones: (A) cover
(B) color
(C) fill
(D) damage
questions.pregunta: 30. The word “bury” in line 26 is closest in meaning to

opciones: (A) to determine the age of other ancient materials that are found with it
(B) as jewelry
(C) to compare the characteristics of living trees to those of ancient trees
(D) to preserve fossil impressions
questions.pregunta: 31. It can be inferred from the passage that amber is most commonly used

opciones: (A) It is composed of an organic material.
(B) It is basically the same as it was millions of years ago.
(C) It may contain the remains of tiny animals.
(D) It is made by a process of mineral replacement.
questions.pregunta: 32. Which of the following is NOT true of amber?

opciones: (A) They were hoping to find a better climate.
(B) They were seeking freedom.
(C) They were following instructions given in a dream.
(D) They were looking for food.
questions.pregunta: 33. According to the passage, why did the first people who came to North America leave their homeland?

opciones: (A) demonstrate reluctance
(B) readily encourage others
(C) have a tendency
(D) often fail
questions.pregunta: 34. The phrase “are easily disposed” in line 12 is closest in meaning to

opciones: (A) frequently
(B) normally
(C) whenever possible
(D) in the end
questions.pregunta: 35. The word “Ultimately” in line 15 is closest in meaning to

opciones: (A) joyous
(B) intricate
(C) competing
(D) forceful
questions.pregunta: 36. The word “compelling” in line 18 is closest in meaning to

opciones: (A) Public performance is essential to verbal art.
(B) Oral narratives are a valid form of literature.
(C) Native Americans have a strong oral tradition in art.
(D) The production of literature provides employment for many artists.
questions.pregunta: 37. What is the main point of the second paragraph?

opciones: (A) They provide financial support for performances.
(B) They judge the quality of the content and presentation.
(C) They participate in the performance by chanting responses.
(D) They determine the length of the performance by requesting a continuation.
questions.pregunta: 38. According to the passage, what responsibility does the audience of a verbal art performance have?

opciones: (A) It reflects historical and contemporary life in Asia.
(B) Its main focus is on daily activities.
(C) It is based primarily on scientific knowledge.
(D) It is reshaped each time it is experienced.
questions.pregunta: 39. What can be inferred about the nature of the Native American literature discussed in the passage?

opciones: (A) It involves acting.
(B) It has ancient origins.
(C) It has a set form.
(D) It expresses an inner vision. 54
questions.pregunta: 40. Which of the following is NOT true of the Native American literature discussed in the passage?

opciones: (A) Written literature reflects social values better than oral literature does.
(B) Written literature involves less interaction between audience and creator during the creative process than oral literature does.
(C) Written literature usually is not based on historical events, whereas oral literature is.
(D) Written literature is not as highly respected as oral literature is.
questions.pregunta: 41. What can be inferred from the passage about the difference between written and oral literature?

opciones: (A) Admiring of its form
(B) Critical of the cost of its production
(C) Amused by its content
(D) Skeptical about its origins
